side_effects: validate_subscription_update_request → checks status is Draft, detects changed fields
persist_subscription_record_update → DynamoDB UpdateItem for changed fields only
Handler returns 204 No Content if changes exist, 200 with 'no changes' message otherwise
mark_payment_as_paid_if_pending → PaymentStatus set to Paid
set_subscription_validity → start_date = now, end_date = now + 30 days
initialize_usage_metric_record → new UsageMetric created (all counters = 0, valid 30 days)
log_subscription_activation → AppActivityLog: actor=Admin, action=ACTIVATE_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD

meta.text:
✏️ Draft Record Updated.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Changed fields validated and persisted to DynamoDB
• Only Draft subscriptions can be edited this way
• No state change — subscription remains in Draft

✅ Subscription Activated.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}} | Plan: {{subscription_plan_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Payment status set to Paid
• start_date = now, end_date = now + 30 days
• New UsageMetric record initialised (all counters = 0, valid 30 days)
• Activation event logged (actor = Admin)

side_effects: log_subscription_suspension → AppActivityLog: actor=Admin, action=SUSPEND_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD (reason from request body)
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted with status = Suspended
Background job detects end_date has passed
log_subscription_expiry → AppActivityLog: actor=System, action=EXPIRE_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted with status = Awaiting Renewal, payment_status = Pending
User access is now blocked — features unavailable until renewal
Prerequisite check: previous extension SIM-EXT-001 is fully consumed (status = Used Up)
create QuotaExtension record SIM-EXT-002 → extended_quota per request, used_quota = zeros, status = Active
save_quota_extension → DynamoDB (ID-collision retry up to 3x)
Quota enforcement: base plan quota + SIM-EXT-002 remaining quota
modify_subscription_record_on_billing_cycle_change → billing_cycle = Annual, payment_status = Paid
set_subscription_end_date_on_billing_cycle_change → end_date += 330 days (Monthly→Annual)
Existing UsageMetric RETAINED — no counter reset
calculate_prorated_payment_for_billing_cycle_upgrade → prorated_amount = annual_price minus credit for unused monthly days
log_billing_cycle_change → AppActivityLog: action=MODIFY_BILLING_CYCLE, data={old=Monthly, new=Annual, payment_info}

meta.text:
➕ Extend Quota — Prerequisite Check.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Status: {{status}}
Active Extension: {{active_extension_id}}

Guard: noActiveExtensionExists
• Pass → creates new QuotaExtension record
• Fail → error: 'An active quota extension already exists. Consume it before creating a new one.'

Enter 'extend' to simulate a passing check, or anything else to simulate rejection.
meta.placeholder: Type 'extend' to proceed, or any other value to see the guard rejection...

side_effects: Guard noActiveExtensionExists passed — no active extension on record
create QuotaExtension SIM-EXT-001 → extended_quota per request, used_quota = zeros, status = Active
save_quota_extension → DynamoDB (ID-collision retry up to 3x)
Quota enforcement: base plan quota + SIM-EXT-001 remaining quota

params.inputError: Guard failed: an active quota extension already exists. Consume it fully before creating a new one.

meta.text:
⬆️ Upgrade Plan — Plan Selection.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Current Plan: {{current_plan_tier}}
Status: {{status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Guard: isNotHighestPlan
• Pass (current plan is Starter or Grow) → upgrades to next tier
• Fail (current plan is Pro) → error: 'Already on the highest plan. Upgrade not available.'

Enter the target plan name to upgrade to (e.g. 'Grow' or 'Pro').
meta.placeholder: Enter target plan: Grow or Pro...

side_effects: Guard isNotHighestPlan passed — current plan is not Pro
modify_subscription_record_on_plan_change → plan_id = upgraded-plan-uuid, payment_status = Paid
set_subscription_end_date_on_plan_change → end_date recalculated per billing cycle
log_subscription_plan_change → AppActivityLog: action=UPGRADE_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD, data={new_plan_id}
Existing UsageMetric RETAINED — quota re-evaluated against new plan automatically
Prorated charge collected from customer for the mid-cycle upgrade

params.inputError: Guard failed: already on the highest plan (Pro). Upgrade is not available.

meta.text:
⬇️ Downgrade Plan — Plan Selection.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Current Plan: {{current_plan_tier}}
Status: {{status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Guard: isLowerPricedPlan
• Pass (selected plan costs less than current) → downgrades
• Fail (selected plan costs same or more) → error: 'Selected plan is not priced lower than the current plan.'

Enter the target plan name to downgrade to (e.g. 'Starter').
meta.placeholder: Enter target plan: Starter or Grow...

side_effects: Guard isLowerPricedPlan passed — selected plan is priced below current plan
modify_subscription_record_on_plan_change → plan_id = starter-plan-uuid, payment_status = Paid
set_subscription_end_date_on_plan_change → end_date = downgrade_date + 30 days
log_subscription_plan_change → AppActivityLog: action=DOWNGRADE_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD, data={new_plan_id}
initialize_usage_metric_record_for_plan_change → new UsageMetric: all counters=0, start=now, end=now+30 days
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted

params.inputError: Guard failed: selected plan is not priced lower than the current plan. Choose a lower-tier plan to downgrade.

meta.text:
⏸️ Subscription Suspended.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Suspension reason recorded in activity log (actor = Admin)
• DynamoDB record persisted with status = Suspended
• User access blocked immediately

⏰ End Date Reached — Awaiting Renewal.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Expiry event logged (actor = System)
• DynamoDB record persisted: status = Awaiting Renewal, payment_status = Pending
• User access blocked — features unavailable until admin renews

➕ Quota Extension Applied.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Extension: {{active_extension_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• New QuotaExtension record created and persisted
• used_quota initialised to zero for all features
• Quota enforcement: base plan quota + sum of all Active extension remaining quotas
• Extension is effective immediately; no expiry date

⬆️ Plan Upgraded.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | New Plan: {{current_plan_tier}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• subscription_plan_id updated to higher-tier plan
• payment_status set to Paid
• end_date recalculated for current billing cycle
• Existing UsageMetric retained — quota limits now evaluated against new plan
• Plan upgrade event logged (actor = Admin)
• Prorated charge collected from customer

📅 Billing Cycle Updated: Monthly → Annual.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• billing_cycle = Annual, payment_status = Paid
• end_date extended by 330 days from current end_date (completing ~1 year)
• Existing UsageMetric RETAINED — no counter reset
• Prorated amount charged (annual_price minus credit for unused monthly days)
• Billing cycle change event logged (actor = Admin)

side_effects: FSM: SuspendedState.activate() → transitions to ActiveState
fsm.update_record_status() → record status = Active
No additional side effects for re-activation from Suspended
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted
Existing dates and UsageMetric unchanged — no reset on re-activation
Background job detects end_date passed while subscription was Suspended
Subscription enters Suspended + Awaiting Renewal edge state
log_subscription_expiry → AppActivityLog: actor=System, action=EXPIRE_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB persisted: payment_status = Pending
Admin must Activate to restore access — status returns to Active on activation

meta.text:
✅ Subscription Re-activated from Suspended.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Status restored to Active
• DynamoDB record persisted
• Existing dates and UsageMetric unchanged — no reset on re-activation

⏰ End Date Reached While Suspended.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: Suspended | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Edge case: end date passed while subscription was already Suspended.
Subscription is now in Suspended + Awaiting Renewal state.

Side effects applied:
• Expiry logged by background job (actor = System)
• payment_status set to Pending
• User remains blocked — must be Activated by admin to restore access

side_effects: FSM: SuspendedState.activate() → transitions to ActiveState
Admin chose to re-activate despite end date having passed
A new billing cycle begins — admin should manually trigger renewal afterwards
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted with status = Active
set_subscription_end_date_on_renewal → end_date = now + 30 days (Monthly) or now + 360 days (Annual)
log_subscription_renewal → AppActivityLog: actor=Admin, action=RENEW_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD
initialize_usage_metric_record_for_renewal → new UsageMetric: all counters=0, start=now, end=now+30 days
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted
log_subscription_activation → AppActivityLog: action=ACTIVATE_SUBSCRIPTION_RECORD (status transitions to Active)
modify_subscription_record_on_billing_cycle_change → billing_cycle = Monthly, payment_status = Paid
set_subscription_end_date_on_billing_cycle_change → end_date = now + 30 days (Annual→Monthly: full reset)
initialize_usage_metric_record_for_billing_cycle_downgrade → new UsageMetric: all counters=0, start=now, end=now+30 days
log_billing_cycle_change → AppActivityLog: action=MODIFY_BILLING_CYCLE, data={old=Annual, new=Monthly, payment_info}
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted
subscription record status = Cancelled (terminal — cannot be reactivated)
update_subscription_record → DynamoDB record persisted
Any active QuotaExtensions become non-utilizable (subscription not Active)

meta.text:
🔁 Subscription Renewed.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• end_date = now + 30 days (Monthly) or now + 360 days (Annual)
• Renewal event logged (actor = Admin)
• New UsageMetric created: all counters = 0, valid 30 days
• DynamoDB record persisted

⬇️ Plan Downgraded.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | New Plan: {{current_plan_tier}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• subscription_plan_id updated to lower-tier plan
• payment_status set to Paid
• end_date = downgrade_date + 30 days
• New UsageMetric created: all counters = 0, valid 30 days
• Plan downgrade event logged (actor = Admin)
• DynamoDB record persisted

📅 Billing Cycle Updated: Annual → Monthly.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Payment: {{payment_status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Activation event logged (status transitions to Active)
• billing_cycle = Monthly, payment_status = Paid
• end_date = now + 30 days (full reset from billing cycle change date)
• New UsageMetric created: all counters = 0, valid 30 days
• Billing cycle change event logged (actor = Admin)
• DynamoDB record persisted

❌ Subscription Cancelled.

ID: {{subscription_id}} | Customer: {{customer_id}}
Status: {{status}} | Billing: {{billing_cycle}}

Side effects applied:
• Subscription record status = Cancelled
• DynamoDB record persisted
• Any active QuotaExtensions become non-utilizable
• Terminal state — this subscription cannot be reactivated
